reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 3 (Code Verification) — Phase 03 incorrectly claims that data columns are pruned after only 2 slots. The 2-slot retention (DATA_COLUMN_RETAIN_DURATION_IN_SLOTS=2 at mutator.rs:93) applies only to the in-memory cache, not the persistent storage layer. Data columns are persisted to disk storage via PersistDataColumnSidecarsTask before cache pruning (mutator.rs:569-577, tasks.rs:519-551). Disk storage pruning uses min_checked_data_availability_epoch() which correctly implements the 4096-epoch retention window (store.rs:3777-3787, using chain_config.min_epochs_for_data_column_sidecars_requests=4096 from config.rs:280). The P2P handler validates requests against this same 4096-epoch window (network.rs:1395). When serving historical data, the code first checks the in-memory cache, then falls back to persistent storage (queries.rs:542-544). The architecture correctly separates hot cache (2 slots) from cold storage (4096 epochs). Spec reference: 01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json lines 831-847, invariant PROP-57888860-inv-032 requires retention for MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs. The implementation is spec-compliant via its two-tier storage architecture.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Sidecars must be retained for at least MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs. forall sidecar: current_epoch - sidecar_epoch <= MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS => sidecar is stored AND served on request
